# Repository: Infinite Canvas React App
# Purpose: React + TypeScript infinite canvas application
# State Management: Legend State v3 beta

This repository contains a React-based infinite canvas application that enables users to pan, zoom, and draw on an unbounded canvas surface. The app is structured to support multiple tools (pen, eraser), layers, and future extensibility.

Directory Structure:
  ├── index.html           - HTML entry point (root, used by Vite)
  ├── public/              - (optional) static assets (served at '/'), e.g., favicon.ico
  ├── docs/
  │   └── adr/             - Architecture Decision Records
  └── src/
      ├── assets/              - static assets imported in code (images, icons, fonts)
      ├── state/               - Legend State v3 beta stores (e.g., canvasStore.ts)
      ├── components/          - reusable UI components
         ├── Canvas/            - core canvas component and drawing helpers
         ├── Toolbar/           - UI controls for drawing tools (pen, eraser, zoom)
         ├── Sidebar/           - optional panels (layers, properties)
         └── common/            - shared UI elements (Buttons, Icons, Modals)
      ├── context/             - React Context providers (CanvasContext)
      ├── hooks/               - custom hooks (usePanZoom, useDraggable)
      ├── utils/               - pure helper functions and math operations
      ├── types/               - shared TypeScript type definitions
      └── styles/              - global styles, CSS reset, theming

Configuration:
  package.json             - dependencies and scripts (dev, build, preview)
  tsconfig.json            - TypeScript settings
  .npmrc                   - pnpm config (package-manager)
  pnpm-lock.yaml           - lockfile generated by pnpm
  .gitignore               - ignored files

Development Workflow:
1. Install dependencies: `pnpm install`
2. Start development server: `pnpm dev`
3. Build for production: `pnpm build`
4. Preview production build: `pnpm preview`

This file provides context for language models and new contributors to understand the repository layout and core components.

## Tech stack

* Front end is React (v19)
* Bundler is Vite (v6)
* State management library is [legend-state](https://github.com/LegendApp/legend-state) v3 beta
  * The document site for Legend State is located in a [separate repository](https://github.com/LegendApp/legend-docs/tree/main/packages/state/src/content/docs)

## Design Decisions

### Canvas Architecture
The canvas implementation uses DOM-based elements rather than Canvas/WebGL rendering:
- Nodes are standard DOM elements with absolute positioning
- Transformations (pan/zoom) use CSS transforms for hardware acceleration
- This approach enables rich interactions, accessibility, and easier styling
- See ADR-002 for detailed reasoning

### Component Architecture
- Core Canvas component manages the viewport and transformation
- CanvasNode components represent individual elements in the canvas
- Components leverage Legend State for efficient updates
- Observer pattern used to minimize re-renders

### Interaction Model
- Custom hooks abstract interaction behaviors:
  - usePanZoom: Manages canvas viewport manipulation
  - useDraggable: Provides element dragging capabilities
- Coordinate systems are clearly separated:
  - Screen coordinates: Relative to viewport
  - Canvas coordinates: Relative to the infinite canvas
- See ADR-003 for details on our hooks-based approach

### Performance Considerations
- CSS transforms used for hardware-accelerated animations
- Fine-grained reactivity with Legend State
- Event handlers optimized to minimize unnecessary calculations
- State updates are batched where possible
